description: Master index of user-research reports, the caseworking screens they captured, and the findings tied to each capture. Order-independent: reports can be ingested backwards in time as pure appends. Per-screen version and ordering are DERIVED at render from reports[].fieldworkDate — never authored. See README.md for how Claude Design consumes it and schema-notes.md for the v2 field reference.

grasslandsChanges.title: Multi-tab caselist reintroduced
grasslandsChanges.change: Three context-switching tabs — "{name}'s cases", "{team} cases" and "All cases" — replace the single simplified list of D1/D2.
grasslandsChanges.why: Multiple-case assignment is needed at scale: up to ~2,000 cases worked by 10 teams of 10+ plus specialist workers, where D1 assumed only a single caseworker.

grasslandsChanges.title: Search and filtering reintroduced
grasslandsChanges.change: A permanent SBI search plus a collapsible Status "filter list" with removable MOJ filter tags on the All cases tab.
grasslandsChanges.why: Needed for managing a large case set.

grasslandsChanges.title: Per-tab subject switchers
grasslandsChanges.change: A "Change assignee" autocomplete on My cases and a "Change team" autocomplete on Open cases.
grasslandsChanges.why: The tab switches let managers focus on individuals, and see the caseload of other teams.

grasslandsChanges.title: Team-based assignment model
grasslandsChanges.change: Cases can be assigned to a team with no owner yet; assigning a case to a person in a team automatically places it in that team's context; Completed cases are always unassigned.
grasslandsChanges.why: Cases assigned to a team let team members self-serve work from that subset.

grasslandsChanges.title: Multi-select bulk assign / reassign
grasslandsChanges.change: Select multiple cases and assign or reassign them in one action, replacing the one-at-a-time assignment of D1/D2.
grasslandsChanges.why: Same scale driver as the multi-tab list — multiple-case assignment is needed for ~2,000 cases across many teams.

grasslandsChanges.title: Full-list server-side sorting
grasslandsChanges.change: Sorting applies across the entire tab list, not just the visible page.
grasslandsChanges.why: This is how the actual live app works.

grasslandsChanges.title: Status colour model
grasslandsChanges.change: Caselist status tags follow a defined colour model.
grasslandsChanges.why: Mirrors the live colour coding: blue = active / no problems, green = positive outcome, grey = not being worked, yellow = on hold, orange = customer ended, red = bad outcome ended.

grasslandsChanges.title: User management for teams & roles
grasslandsChanges.change: Create user / team / role, the Change flows, the team-picker dropdown, and condition-gated deletes.
grasslandsChanges.why: Needed to support the new tabs and team/role-specific tasks.

caselist.title: Caselist
caselist.summary: The caseworker list of grant applications. Tested as a tabbed status list (Jan 2025), simplified to a single list for D1/D2, and now reintroduced in Grasslands with My cases / {team} cases / All cases tabs, search and filtering.

tasklist.title: Tasks – Tasklist
tasklist.summary: The Tasks tab of a case — the list of review tasks and automated checks, and (once tasks are accepted) the caseworker Decision radio group (Approve / Put on hold / Return to customer / Reject / Withdraw) with a compulsory explanation for auditing.

task-page.title: Tasks – Task page
task-page.summary: An individual task within a case (e.g. an eligibility or quality check) — its detail, the checks to make, and the caseworker's decision on that task.

confirmation.title: Confirmation page
confirmation.summary: The amend / return decision flow: the 'are you sure?' interstitials and the outcome pages for amending or returning an application before agreement, under the withdraw-and-duplicate model.

application.title: Application
application.summary: The submitted application detail within a case — the applicant's answers and submitted information.

calculations.title: Calculations
calculations.summary: The grant calculations / payment working within a case.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.summary: The case history timeline — events and contact shown in chronological order.

notes.title: Notes
notes.summary: Internal case notes added by caseworkers.

agreement.title: Agreement
agreement.summary: The grant agreement issued once a case is approved.

documents.title: Documents
documents.summary: The documents / evidence tab — supporting files grouped by type (licences, accounts, plans, permissions, quotes).

assigned-users.title: Assigned users
assigned-users.summary: The assigned-users tab — who the case is assigned to, with assignment history.

land-details.title: Land details
land-details.summary: The land / mapping detail view for a case — a parcel map with highlighted land and a 'Parcel and area' table (area, intersections such as SSSI / historic features, percentage holding). Designed for SFI processors; not yet deployed.

messages.title: Messages
messages.summary: Messaging with the applicant or agent, with a full message history and template copy. Explored in Jan 2025; not yet deployed.

//versions: A screen's history is its design VERSIONS (states over time), sorted by sortDate. Research captures attach to a version via screenId@date; a version may have no capture (design-only) and still renders. Order/label derived at render — never authored.

versions.changeReason: First tested concept — a status-tabbed case list (New / Not started / In progress / Complete / All) with select-and-assign.
Richer list explored for D1 (Your/Team/All tabs, Priority/Stage/Status + RFI flag, search & filter). Deferred for delivery/scope.
Simplified single list shipped for D1 — one list, one-at-a-time assignment, no search or filter (delivery & scope management).
Grasslands: rich list reintroduced for scale (My / {team} / All tabs, search, status filter, bulk assign, team model). Not yet tested.
First tested concept — the case view Tasks tab with automated checks (Companies House / Spotlight / HMRC) and the application/quality task list.
Tasks tab became 'Application review' with a Decision control (Pass / Request more information / Recommend rejection), in-context guidance, and SFI case-management issues.
Decision set expanded (Approve / Put on hold / Amend / Return to customer / Reject / Withdraw) and the amend/return flow added — new functionality rolled out and deployed quickly.
First tested concept — a Documents tab grouping supporting evidence (licences, accounts, plans, permissions, quotes).
First tested concept — an Assigned-users tab to assign a case and view assignment history.
First tested concept — a Messages tab to message the applicant with a message history. Not deployed.
Land details map explored for SFI processors (parcel map + intersections table). Deferred — out of scope for D1.
Amend / return confirmation flow (the withdraw-and-duplicate model) — new functionality rolled out and deployed quickly.

//personas: Persona registry (like screens). needs[] join a persona to the screens that should serve it. Populated from the UR 'User Needs Summary' (Confluence, Jun 2025). A need is only 'met' with an evidence link.

farmer.name: Farmer or farming agent
farmer.role: Applies for and claims grant funding
farmer.summary: Runs an agricultural business under cost, supply-chain and climate pressure; needs to find, apply for and claim grants with minimal disruption and cost — and often feels forced to hire an agent.

grant-appraiser.name: Grant appraiser
grant-appraiser.summary: Works a queue of cases; needs tools and consistent guidance to assess efficiently, focus on strong applications and decide quickly and safely — with less manual admin.

workflow-manager.name: Workflow manager / Team lead
workflow-manager.role: Allocates and oversees a team's cases
workflow-manager.summary: Needs visibility and control over team workload and case progress to allocate fairly by complexity, track holds and avoid processing delays.

sfi-processor.name: SFI processor
sfi-processor.summary: Handles land-based SFI cases; needs a reliable system, parcel-level land data, clear notes and hold tracking, and smart search/status to process without rework.

policy-staff.name: Policy & scheme-design staff
policy-staff.summary: Needs visibility into likely volumes, eligibility and uptake to plan budgets and shape schemes that deliver environmental and policy outcomes.

needs.statement: I need to discover relevant, timely funding opportunities, so I don't miss out on support.
I need to know whether my project is eligible before I invest time, so I don't waste effort on unsuitable applications.
I need clear steps, timelines and evidence requirements, so I can apply successfully without hiring an agent.
I need to see how my application is progressing, so I can plan with confidence.
I need to change or withdraw an application in progress, so I can accommodate changes to my project.
I need a timely, transparent decision, so I can make financial and operational plans.
I need clear guidance on claim evidence and submission, so my claim is processed quickly.
I need to know when and where payment will be made, so I can plan my finances.
I need to easily find and return to my active cases, so I work efficiently and don't lose track.
I need to prioritise by deadline and urgency, so I meet KPIs.
I need the full case history and customer responses in one place, so I can resolve issues without trawling systems.
I need to apply checks using consistent, accessible guidance, so I assess accurately.
I need to reduce duplicate data entry and admin, so I can focus on decisions.
I need to record a decision in one obvious place, so I don't miss a step.
I need to spot when an applicant has made multiple applications, so only one goes through appraisal.
I need work allocated to me fairly and transparently, so my workload stays manageable.
I need to see what each team member is working on, so I can manage workload.
I need to monitor the status of all active projects, so I can prevent delays.
I need to filter by scheme type and deadline, so I can manage peaks and prioritise support.
I need to understand case complexity, so I can allocate work fairly and match it to capacity.
I need to track holds and customer action points, so I can unblock progress.
I need to allocate and reassign cases, so resources match demand.
I need a reliable, user-friendly case management system, so I can process without crashes or manual workarounds.
I need parcel-level land data inside the case, so I can check eligibility without leaving the system.
I need end-to-end case visibility with clear notes and hold tracking, so I reduce rework and enable smooth handovers.
I need smart search, filters and clear status indicators, so I can quickly find and prioritise the right cases.
I need clear, up-to-date guidance and access to expert support, so I can process accurately and confidently.
I need input into changes and early access to test systems, so schemes aren't launched before they're ready.
I need visibility of likely application volumes and uptake, so I can plan budgets and resources.
I need to see whether applications meet policy aims and deliver the best environmental outcomes, so I can shape schemes.

reports.title: Future Grants — RPA internal user problems
reports.author: Nick Buckland
reports.system: DEFRA Future Grants — RPA caseworker process for FTF / FETF grants (as-is)
reports.participants: 2 FTF appraisers, 2 FETF appraisers, 1 FTF SME, 1 Executive Officer, 1 Higher Executive Officer
reports.confidence: Indicative discovery — small qualitative sample (n=7), partial role coverage, findings are interpretative. Directional, not representative.
reports.sourceFile: FCP UR Copy of Future Grants _ RPA Research _ Summary 060824.pptx
reports.headline: RPA caseworkers process FTF/FETF grants through Giles plus a sprawl of Excel/Word — most applications fail first time and manual checking, fragmented guidance and poor system integration drive delays and errors.

themes.title: Most grant applications fail first time
themes.detail: Staff estimate 90%+ of FTF and ~60% of FETF applications need further correspondence — missing documents, wrong formats, missing invoices/match-funding evidence, missing RPA registration or permissions, FETF items not matching the category spec.

themes.title: Applicants do not understand what is required of them
themes.detail: They don't know preferred formats, that they must be registered, how permissions work, what evidence is acceptable, or how much detail to give.

themes.title: Application forms are too complicated
themes.detail: Complex and confusing for applicants; much technical content is set by other bodies (e.g. the Environment Agency) and defended as "as complex as it needs to be".

themes.title: Most of the work is done in Excel and Word
themes.detail: Cases are logged in Giles, but active checks and scoring run in Excel/Word on SharePoint (ECR, scoring spreadsheet, tracker) with heavy copy-paste and duplication.

themes.title: Manual processes require time-consuming manual checking
themes.detail: Laborious checking and re-checking across many documents and guidance; manual steps also cause small human errors. "We have to take screenshots of everything."

themes.title: Systems are inflexible to different digital formats
themes.detail: Incompatible supporting-information formats cause delays; e.g. third-party transfer links (WeTransfer) can't be accepted.

themes.title: Internal guidance can be overwhelming
themes.detail: Guidance is spread across many places (RPA hub, Word, Excel, SharePoint); staff keep several guidance pages open at once.

themes.title: RPA staff cover multiple schemes, which can be challenging
themes.detail: Non-specialists need a working knowledge of many schemes and find it hard to stay on top of them all.

themes.title: There is a lack of integration with RPA systems and other organisations
themes.detail: Many checks are manual — RPA call centre, registration/permissions, Companies House, dual-funding checks with other arm's-length bodies; disconnected CRM.

themes.title: The 'clock stop' mechanism may incentivise delays
themes.detail: Appraisers have 60 (FTF) / 30 (FETF) working days; stopping the clock for missing info resets the KPI and may be used to manage busy periods.

themes.title: Technical checks usually cause significant delays
themes.detail: Slurry applications need Environment Agency checks, typically 2–9+ months.

themes.title: Application windows and seasonality cause volume spikes
themes.detail: Tight application windows and the farming calendar create demand spikes that are hard to staff.

themes.title: Concerns that agents submit poor-quality applications
themes.detail: Most FTF/FETF applications come via agents; staff perceive these as poorer quality and sometimes unresponsive.

reports.title: Future Grants — general research themes (farmers & agents)
reports.author: Nick Buckland
reports.system: DEFRA Future Grants — FETF apply / claims (farmer & agent research)
reports.method: 13 × 1-hour qualitative interviews (feedback on new FETF apply / claims designs; general-themes synthesis)
reports.participants: 9 farmers (large >£1m, medium £50k–£500k, small <£50k turnover) and 4 farming agents; contributor Naomi Wilcock
reports.confidence: Indicative qualitative — 13 in-depth interviews, not statistically generalisable; self-reported and interpretative. Illuminates patterns, not population estimates.
reports.sourceFile: Future Grants _ General Research Themes _ Summary 181124.pptx
reports.headline: Farmers and agents see the grants landscape as too complex and fragmented post-BPS, distrust the RPA/Defra, and many can't apply without agent help — with consistent pain across discovering, applying for and claiming grants.

themes.title: Macro pressures shape how farmers consume grants
themes.detail: Global-market and supply-chain pressure, high input costs, high debt, low cash ("asset rich, cash poor"), climate impacts and frequent grant changes mean farmers seek funding to stay viable.

themes.title: Grant schemes feel too complex — and increasingly so
themes.detail: The shift from BPS ("simple, one form, one payment a year") to many unfamiliar, changing schemes requiring separate applications for much the same information.

themes.title: The grants & payments landscape is complicated
themes.detail: Too many schemes; similar, confusing, unmemorable names; overlapping criteria; complex and inconsistent application requirements between schemes.

themes.title: Many farmers can't apply without an agent
themes.detail: Complexity drives reliance on agents — "90% of the time we are doing the application for them" — who are both frustrated by and commercially reliant on that complexity.

themes.title: Low trust — RPA/Defra seen as disconnected and high-risk
themes.detail: Requirements feel too prescriptive and not feasible; a perception that the RPA looks for fault and withholds money. "Any engagement with the RPA is a risk."

themes.title: Farming is localised and contextual
themes.detail: Schemes are felt to ignore the local, contextual detail farmers (as custodians) know best; participants favour a more collaborative, less prescriptive approach.

themes.title: A significant group are "analogue by default" — risk of digital exclusion
themes.detail: Paper records, low digital skills, limited hardware and help. "A lot of the farmers I know will not be able to do this… they won't know how to digitise the paperwork."

themes.title: Informal, localised supply chains
themes.detail: Second-hand kit, preferred local suppliers, trusted help and the recycling of BPS money — flexible purchasing requirements are needed.

themes.title: Perception that grants favour larger, intensive operators
themes.detail: Larger farms can afford consultants, in-house research and upfront capital; smaller operators feel disadvantaged.

themes.title: Consistent pain across the journey — discover, decide, apply, claim
themes.detail: Hard to find/understand schemes; eligibility and scoring confusing; repeated information; not all journeys start in the RPA portal; restrictive windows out of sync with the farming calendar; confusing item specs/codes; payment delays and cashflow strain; no claim-status visibility; all-or-nothing claims.

reports.title: FCP themes — challenges in the grants space
reports.author: Not stated in the deliverable
reports.system: Farming and Countryside Programme (FCP) — applicant / grants discovery
reports.method: Qualitative research with farmers and advisors / agents
reports.participants: Farmers and advisors / agents (sample size not stated in the deliverable)
reports.confidence: Discovery-phase thematic synthesis, captured from a single summary slide with no sample size or method detail. Treat as directional context, not measured findings.
reports.headline: Farmers and advisors find the grants space hard to navigate — analogue by default, low trust in Defra/RPA, confusing schemes and overwhelming guidance — the discovery backdrop to the caseworking work.

themes.title: Many farming processes are analogue by default
themes.detail: Smaller operators say their processes are paper-based and they would struggle to digitise them.

themes.title: Low trust in Defra and RPA among farmers and advisors
themes.detail: Participants feel Defra and RPA are difficult to work with and detached from the realities of farming.

themes.title: It is easy to confuse different schemes
themes.detail: Participants frequently struggle to differentiate between schemes.

themes.title: Grant benefit skews towards larger operators
themes.detail: Many feel the complexity of grant schemes favours larger, more intensive operators.

themes.title: Applying for grants feels onerous
themes.detail: Many farmers say the proposed process feels more onerous than previous processes.

themes.title: It is difficult to understand eligibility and scoring
themes.detail: Profitable farms rely on agents to decipher the rules; smaller operators can feel left behind.

themes.title: Grant guidance is overwhelming
themes.detail: Participants feel existing guidance is verbose and difficult to understand.

themes.title: Grant application windows make planning difficult
themes.detail: Timelines are tight and the forward look is limited.

reports.title: Usability testing of RPA case management designs
reports.author: Nick Buckland
reports.system: DEFRA Future Grants — RPA case management (caseworker prototype) for FETF / FTF grants
reports.method: 10 × 1-hour moderated usability tests on a clickable Figma prototype
reports.participants: RPA grant-appraisal staff: EO Appraisers, HEO Workflow Managers, HEO Subject Matter Experts (SMEXs). Contributors: Chas Linn, Naomi Wilcock, Rebekah Broughton, Mark Stead, Garry Prior
reports.confidence: Indicative usability study — 10 moderated sessions on an early concept prototype; qualitative and directional (reactions to early designs, not validated outcomes). Further research flagged for status terminology and messaging.
reports.sourceFile: Future Grants _ RPA Case Management _ Summary 280125.pptx
reports.headline: RPA staff were overwhelmingly positive about a consolidated Case List and Individual Case View prototype — keen on automation, one-place information and role-specific views — with refinements needed to status terminology, an actions column, applicant details, and notes/messages.

reports.title: Research with RPA Appraisers (case management debrief)
reports.author: Nick Buckland
reports.system: DEFRA Future Grants — RPA case management for Farming Transformation Fund (FTF), heading into MVP
reports.stage: Alpha → MVP definition (private beta targeted Sept 2025)
reports.method: Mixed-method: 8 in-depth qualitative interviews + a quantitative survey of 37 respondents
reports.participants: Interviews: EO Appraisers, HEO Workflow Managers, SMEXs. Survey (n=37): Appraisers, Workflow Managers, SMEXs and senior management
reports.confidence: Indicative — 8 depth interviews plus a 37-person survey across a small, FTF-focused operational team. The survey adds quantitative weight but the population is small; treat percentages as directional priorities, not precise measures.
reports.headline: FTF appraisers and workflow managers rate current workload processes 3.35/5 — top frustrations are manual trackers/spreadsheets, unfair opaque allocation, QC/panel bottlenecks, scattered guidance and poor customer comms; they most want applicant evidence upload (68%), automated checks (46%) and in-system messaging (43%). MVP private beta targeted Sept 2025.

themes.title: Manual processes and lack of automation (top frustration)
themes.detail: Heavy reliance on trackers, spreadsheets and manual checks. "Zero automation, no case management function, reliant on trackers to support." Staff want automated tasks and integrated systems.

themes.title: Unfair, opaque work allocation
themes.detail: Cases allocated without notice or regard to existing workload/complexity; KPIs distorted by RFIs dropping priority. "Work is allocated to you even if you have a lot of cases already." Want fair, transparent allocation with manager visibility of complexity and workload.

themes.title: Process delays & bottlenecks
themes.detail: Long waits for QCs, SMEx advice and panel decisions; KPI clock resets seen as gaming the system. Want streamlined approvals and fewer unnecessary checks — "one process where you do everything and get it QC'd at the end."

themes.title: Complex, scattered guidance
themes.detail: Lengthy ECR/SIG guidance, easy to miss points; near-universal RFIs are taken as evidence the process is too hard. Want clear, task-aligned guidance and checklists, ideally embedded in the system.

themes.title: Poor customer communication & progress visibility
themes.detail: Constant back-and-forth email, blank Word/Excel documents that invite errors, an outdated email-inbox interface. Want applicant upload, pre-filled documents, clearer prompts and shared status visibility.

themes.title: Most-wanted features
themes.detail: Applicants upload evidence directly (68%), automated 3rd-party checks (46%), direct messaging with applicants and full message history (43% each), clear deadlines/reminders (32%), end-to-end progress tracking to payment (30%), assign/reassign a case (16%).

themes.title: How staff find cases — search & filters
themes.detail: Staff search first by Project reference (89%), then SBI (62%), business name and applicant name. Most-helpful filters: project reference, SBI, appraiser, grant name, KPI status, case status.

themes.title: Status labels staff want
themes.detail: A rich lifecycle vocabulary: Unassigned, Application received, ECR / Full appraisal in progress, Awaiting customer info (RFI), Awaiting SMEx advice, On clock stop, Awaiting/in QC, Approved, Contracted, Rejected, Withdrawn, Closed, Rework required — plus indicators (KPI status, case owner, linked cases).

themes.title: Distinct Appraiser vs Workflow Manager needs
themes.detail: Appraisers: find/return to active projects, see urgent actions and deadlines, know when a customer responded, one-place overview, less manual entry. Managers: see each member's workload, track all active projects, filter by scheme/deadline, judge case complexity for fair allocation, monitor on-hold cases.

reports.title: Case management debrief — SFI Processors (combined survey)
reports.author: Nick Buckland
reports.system: DEFRA Future Grants — RPA case management across FTF Appraisers and SFI Processors, heading into MVP
reports.stage: Alpha → MVP definition (private beta targeted Sept 2025)
reports.method: Mixed-method: combined RPA workload survey (n=76, FTF Appraisers + SFI Processors) for feature & filter questions; qualitative base (8 FTF interviews) shared with the FTF-only debrief
reports.participants: FTF Appraisers, HEO Workflow Managers, SMEXs, SFI Processors and senior management (combined survey n=76)
reports.confidence: Indicative — combined survey n=76 broadens the quantitative view across FTF Appraisers and SFI Processors, but qualitative depth (n=8 interviews) and open-text frustrations remain FTF-focused (n=37). Directional cross-team priorities.
reports.headline: Extending the workload survey to SFI Processors (n=76) confirms priorities hold across teams: staff most want everything about a case in one place (88%) and end-to-end progress tracking; the strongest case-list filters are SBI (87%), Case status (78%) and Application reference (72%).

themes.title: Priorities hold across FTF Appraisers and SFI Processors
themes.detail: Extending the survey to SFI processors (n=76 combined) validates the FTF debrief — the same frustrations and feature priorities recur across both teams, supporting a single case-management design usable by both.

themes.title: "Everything in one place" is the dominant feature ask (n=76)
themes.detail: Having all information in one place 88%; track progress application→payment 45%; see full history of messages and actions 43%; direct messaging 25%; clear deadlines/reminders 22%; assign/reassign a case 12%.

themes.title: Strongest case-list filters: SBI, Case status, Application reference (n=76)
themes.detail: SBI 87%, Case status 78%, Application reference 72%, then who it's assigned to 46%, applicant name 46%, submission date 40%, business name 38%, due date 28%, applicant location 4%.

themes.title: Same core frustrations as the FTF debrief
themes.detail: Manual processes & lack of automation, unfair/opaque allocation, QC/panel bottlenecks, scattered guidance and poor customer comms — shared with the 10 June appraiser debrief and not re-counted here (open-text base n=37).

themes.title: Same desired solutions and role needs
themes.detail: Automation, process streamlining, clearer embedded guidance, customer upload/pre-fill, and fair allocation with manager visibility; distinct Appraiser vs Workflow-Manager needs — carried in the companion report.

themes.title: Heading to MVP — aligned across teams
themes.detail: Concurrent FTF + SFI research aims to align case-management designs across both teams; MVP working private beta targeted September 2025.

reports.title: RPA case management — design history + SFI processor research
reports.author: Naomi Wilcock, Nick Buckland, Chas Linn
reports.system: DEFRA Future Grants — reusable RPA case management (FTF + SFI), heading into SFI private beta Day 1 (Sept 2025)
reports.stage: Alpha → MVP (HTML prototype on GOV.UK + MOJ Design System)
reports.method: Design-history synthesis (Jan–June 2025) + SFI processor research: 8 in-depth interviews + an 84-respondent survey
reports.participants: SFI processors — AO Appraisers and Workflow Managers (interviews); AOs, EOs and senior management (survey n=84). Earlier phases: RPA grant appraisers
reports.confidence: Mixed — the design-history narrative is a factual record of the team's own work; the SFI processor findings are indicative (8 interviews + 84 survey across a stressed, SitiAgri-dependent team). Directional priorities, not precise measures.
reports.headline: The June 2025 HTML iteration evolved the case list (Team tab, Priority/Stage/Status + RFI flags, search & filter) and case view (Application review + decision recording, guidance, customer contact) and introduced a Land details map; an 84-person SFI survey reaffirmed SBI/case-status/application-reference filters and 'everything in one place' (88%).

reports.title: Amendments to grant applications pre agreement
reports.author: Janice Hannaway
reports.system: SFI Reform — Manage rural grant applications (caseworker prototype)
reports.method: Qualitative in-depth interviews + moderated prototype usability testing
reports.confidence: Indicative, not definitive — small qualitative sample. Treat as hypotheses and design directions, not a representative view.
reports.sourceFile: Amendments to grant appliations pre agreement.pptx
reports.headline: Caseworkers could navigate the prototype, but the "withdraw + duplicate application" model causes real anxiety and confusion and needs redesigning.

images.state: Your team's applications — tabbed by status, with select-and-Assign

findings: Coverage: the case list was tested across its status tabs (New, Not started, In progress, Complete, All); all were understood, with the comments below being the only issues recorded.
All participants understood the case-list view and every column heading, and felt able to organise cases by priority and status.
Particularly liked the priority column (priority level + days left to hit the KPI); some wanted KPI days-remaining shown across all cases.
Felt similar to other case-management systems, especially Universal Credit.

Role-specific views seen as essential: EO Appraisers want their assigned cases and priorities; HEO Workflow Managers a team overview by urgency against the 60-day KPI; HEO SMEXs to filter by specialism; SEOs team-level performance and KPI analysis.

Status terminology ('in progress', 'on hold', 'ready for QC', 'approved', 'rejected', 'withdrawn') was important but a source of confusion; some felt completed cases were redundant and preferred focusing on active cases.

recommendedChanges.text: Add an 'Actions' column showing the last action / outstanding actions / new messages per case.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live caselist has no actions/last-action column.

recommendedChanges.text: Show applicant details alongside the project name to add context and spot multiple applications from one client.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live caselist shows a Business (applicant) column alongside the case ID and SBI.

recommendedChanges.text: Provide role-specific views (appraiser's own cases; manager team overview; SMEX specialisation filter; SEO performance).
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live caselist has three role-tailored tabs — the user's own cases ({name}'s cases), team cases ({team} cases) and All cases — plus team/assignee/status filters. Appraiser and manager views exist, but no SMEX specialisation filter or SEO performance analytics.

recommendedChanges.text: Refine and clarify case-status terminology to align with future workflows.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live caselist uses a defined status set with coloured tags, but the deck flagged ongoing research need on labels.

recommendedChanges.text: Show KPI days-remaining / priority across all cases in the list.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live caselist has no priority or KPI-days column.

recommendedChanges.text: Add flags & notifications for outstanding/new actions (new messages, status changes).

images.state: Tasks tab — full application tasks & automated checks (Companies House / Spotlight / HMRC)

findings: Coverage: the full case view was walked — Tasks, Application, Land, Documents, Notes, Messages, Timeline and Assigned users. Comments were recorded on the tabs that have their own screens below; the rest were tested with no issues recorded.
Appreciated the layout and look and feel; the main value was seeing everything about a case in one place without navigating folders.
Automated checks (reconciling business info with HMRC / Companies House / Spotlight) seen as a significant improvement over current processes.
The timeline feature was well received — contact history without trawling SharePoint.

images.state: Documents tab — grouped evidence (licences, accounts, plans, permissions, quotes)

findings: Access to uploaded documents and supporting evidence was appreciated.
Wanted to categorise and relabel evidence for consistency and ease of scanning, and to archive older documents.

recommendedChanges.text: Let caseworkers categorise, relabel and archive uploaded evidence.

images.state: Assigned users tab — assign and view previous assignees

findings: The 'Assigned users' tab was less useful, particularly for Appraisers.

images.state: Messages tab — message the applicant + message history

findings: Notes vs messages: the distinction was unclear; frequent use of template copy in emails suggested building templates into the design.
The design assumed messages go to the applicant, but many cases are handled by an agent — communication preferences (applicant vs agent) are needed.

recommendedChanges.text: Clarify the distinction between internal notes and external messages (explore alternative labels).
Provide template copy for common case communications.
Support communication preferences — send messages to the applicant or their agent.
Provide a way to record and track telephone contact with applicants.

captures.note: Design exploration for D1 (June 2025): a richer case list with Your / Team / All applications tabs, separate Priority, Stage and Status columns plus an RFI Flag column, a Reassign case action, and a search + filter panel. Why it didn't ship as-is: the FRPS-PB-D1 build was deliberately simplified for delivery and scope-management reasons — D1 shipped a single list, one-at-a-time assignment, and no search or filtering. These richer features were kept as deferred proposals; D2 also kept the simplified list, and they are being reintroduced only now in Grasslands (untested).
captures.rationale: Simplified for D1 delivery and scope management: one list, one-at-a-time assignment, no search/filter. The richer design stayed deferred through D2 and is being reintroduced only in Grasslands.

images.state: Iterated case list — Your/Team/All applications tabs, Priority·Stage·Status·Flag columns, RFI flags
New search & filter — search by project ID/business/status/assignee; filter by Grant/Stage/Status/Assignee

findings: Iteration driven by co-design and survey insights: new columns, a Team applications tab for managers, clearer priority/stage/status labels and RFI flags, and new search & filter.

SFI processors confirmed they need quick access to SBI, application reference, business name, case status, priority, key dates and who the case is assigned to. "Especially useful if there's more than one application for an SBI."
Managers rely on filters (KPI risk, scheme type, stage) to allocate and monitor; appraisers prefer minimal filters due to smaller caseloads.
Survey priorities (n=84): top filters SBI 87%, case status 78%, application reference 72%, assignee 46%, applicant name 46%.

recommendedChanges.text: Role-tailored tabs (own / team / all applications).
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live caselist has three role-tailored tabs: the user's own cases ({name}'s cases), team cases ({team} cases) and All cases.

recommendedChanges.text: Search by project ID / business name / status / assignee and filter by Grant / Stage / Status / Assignee.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live caselist has SBI search and Status, Team and Assignee filters; not yet Grant/Stage filters or full multi-field search.

recommendedChanges.text: Separate Priority, Stage and Status columns with RFI / workflow flags.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live caselist shows Status (with coloured tags) but no separate Priority/Stage columns or RFI Flag column.

captures.note: June 2025 HTML iteration: the Tasks tab became an 'Application review' with a review checklist, a conflict-of-interest warning, in-context guidance ('Application review checklist guide'), and the first decision-recording control — a Decision radio (Pass / Request more information / Recommend rejection). The SFI variant surfaced case-management 'issues' (eligibility check, available-area calculation, SSSI consent), each with a 'Take action' button. These case-view screens shipped in D1. Out of scope for D1: the Messages tab and the Land tab (both deferred).
captures.rationale: Case view shipped in D1 with decision recording, embedded guidance and the SFI case-management issues. Messages and Land tabs were out of scope for D1.

images.state: Application review — checklist + Decision radio (Pass / Request more information / Recommend rejection)
SFI case view — review actions (eligibility, available area, SSSI consent) with Take action
In-context guidance — Application review checklist guide

findings: Consolidating everything about a case in one place remained the dominant ask (88% of the n=84 SFI survey).

SFI processors want to flag checks/issues ('case managements'), a clear list of what needs checking, visual identification of which land parcels need review (SSSI, Inheritance Tax Exemption), a timeline of how each issue was resolved, a case history log, and full message history with the applicant.
The new Decision control (Pass / Request more information / Recommend rejection) introduced explicit decision-recording on a case/task.

recommendedChanges.text: Provide a way to record a decision on a case or task.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: The live case view carries a caseworker Decision radio group (expanded since to put on hold / amend / return / reject / withdraw).

recommendedChanges.text: Surface case-management 'issues' with per-issue actions and which land parcels need review.
Show a timeline of issue resolution and a full case history log.

captures.rationale: Out of scope for D1 — the Land tab was deferred from the FRPS-PB-D1 build. This is the design as explored, not shipped in D1.

images.state: Parcel map with highlighted land + Parcel and area table (area, intersections, percentage holding)

findings: Out of scope for D1 — the Land tab was deferred from the FRPS-PB-D1 build; the design below is what was explored, not what shipped.
Introduced for SFI processors, who need parcel-level land data when assessing applications.
Existing mapping (SitiAgri, Defra Magic Maps) is broadly adequate but should be integrated into a single case-management system.

Desired data layers: land use, land cover, recent customer-submitted changes, dual-funding evidence; parcel intersections with SSSI, historic features, flood risk, scheduled monuments, SHINE, Inheritance Tax Exemption, moorland line, GIXX.

recommendedChanges.text: Integrate mapping into the case-management system rather than separate tools.
Surface the desired land-data layers and parcel intersections (SSSI, ITE, flood risk, etc.).
Confirm which land data to surface for SFI Day 1.

captures.note: Building on the June-2025 'Application review' design — which introduced a Decision radio (Pass / Request more information / Recommend rejection) and in-context guidance — D2 expanded the decision set (Approve / Put on hold / Amend / Return to customer / Reject / Withdraw) and added the amend / return flow. Why the change: this amend / return / withdraw functionality was new — rolled out and deployed quickly, ahead of full design refinement, which is why this study surfaced confusion around the withdraw-and-duplicate model.
captures.rationale: Amend / return / withdraw was new functionality rolled out and deployed quickly; design refinement (this study's findings) came afterwards.
findings: All participants found and used "Return to customer" and "Amend" with minimal prompting and understood their purpose.
The radio decision set (put on hold / amend / return / reject / withdraw) was generally intuitive.

Mental-model mismatch: participants expected to amend via the Application view or land/mapping tasks (as in Siti Agri), not a generic decision radio. One navigated to the Application tab first; the other two found the right option after exploring the task list.

recommendedChanges.text: Reconcile the decision-radio model with the caseworker expectation of amending from the Application / land view.
Make clearer which decisions a caseworker can take themselves vs escalate (embed the 10% threshold / escalation guidance).

captures.rationale: The amend / return confirmation flow uses a withdraw-and-duplicate model — new functionality rolled out and deployed quickly; the anxiety and confusion this study found are a consequence of that fast rollout, not a settled design.

images.state: Amend — "Are you sure you want to amend this application?" interstitial
Return — "Are you sure you want to return this application to the customer?" interstitial
Amend outcome — withdrawn, amend in Grants-UI
Return outcome — application returned to customer for amending

findings: Confusion and anxiety about the current application being "withdrawn" and the amendment being a separate "duplicate application". "Withdraw" read as the same as "reject"; participants feared irreversible / "ghost" applications blocking land parcels.
Unclear whether the duplicate would be pre-populated with the original information, and whether the original stays visible/editable. "Grants-UI" terminology not understood.
Return: making the applicant redo an entire application felt frustrating, as completing one can take several days.

The outcome pages felt confusing; participants wanted a view of what changed between the old and new application, and worried that repeated amendments would stack up application/case numbers and confuse applicants and staff.
Restart-all-tasks messaging raised workload concerns, especially for complex applications (80+ checks).
Quotes: "I see withdraw and reject as same thing" (P1); "Changing case numbers will make this real confusing… they are just going to stack up" (P1).

recommendedChanges.text: Replace "withdraw" with clearer language to avoid "ghost application" anxiety.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live amend-confirm still reads "withdraw the current application".

recommendedChanges.text: Explicitly state what happens to the withdrawn record and the caseworker's work, and whether forms are pre-populated.
Simplify "Grants-UI" to plain language (participants suggested "duplicate application").
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: "Grants-UI" still used verbatim on the live screen.

recommendedChanges.text: Clarify data retention for the customer-facing resubmission (pre-populated vs from scratch, what happens to the original).
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live return-confirm now reads "close this current application making it view only" and "create a new identical application for the customer to review, amend and re-submit" — the original's fate and pre-population are now stated; "Withdraw" has been dropped on this screen.

recommendedChanges.text: Reframe so a return for amendment does not read as a full restart.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: Live screen still says "require casework to be started from scratch on the newly submitted case".

recommendedChanges.text: Show what changed between original and amended application (diff view), essential for large multi-parcel cases.
recommendedChanges.resolvedNote: No diff/change view on the live confirmation page.

recommendedChanges.text: Rethink case-number proliferation across repeated amendments.
Clear task-restart logic so caseworkers understand exactly what needs re-doing vs what is preserved.
